repositories.brief_summary: Week 8 saw significant progress with 24 merged PRs, including the major addition of exhaustiveness checking for ocamllex regular expressions, new Array functions (fold_left2/fold_right2), and extensive stdlib documentation improvements. The week also included important runtime improvements, GC enhancements, and build system fixes.
repositories.new_features_summary: Major new features include exhaustiveness checking for ocamllex regular expressions (#14448), new Array.fold_left2 and Array.fold_right2 functions (#14553), and improved runtime clarity with Scannable_* macros (#14493). The Iarray API was updated to use Iarray.t consistently (#14576).
new_features: **Exhaustiveness checking for ocamllex regular expressions** - If a rule does not match some input, an example of nonmatching input is shown in a warning message. The new warning can be disabled using `-w -missing-case`. [#14448](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14448)

**Array.fold_left2 and Array.fold_right2 functions** - New standard library functions that fold over two arrays simultaneously, raising Invalid_argument if arrays have different lengths. [#14553](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14553)

**Improved runtime clarity with Scannable_* macros** - Replaced all comparisons against No_scan_tag with macros for better legibility and correctness, fixing one bug in weak.c. [#14493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14493)

**Iarray API consistency** - Updated Iarray module to expose 'a Iarray.t in function types rather than 'a iarray, making the built-in iarray type an implementation detail. [#14576](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14576)
**Improved installation time in opam** - Shrinking Git-generated source archives to reduce installation time. [#14152](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14152)
**Fix for --disable-unix-lib build** - ocamltest now builds correctly when configured with --disable-unix-lib. [#14558](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14558)

**Linux ppc64 architecture detection** - Proper detection for Linux distros targeting ELFv1 (like Debian), correctly setting the arch configure variable. [#14573](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14573)
repositories.activity_summary: The week saw 24 PRs merged, 18 new PRs opened, 5 new issues created, and 4 issues closed. Notable merged work includes stdlib improvements (documentation fixes, new Array functions), runtime enhancements (GC improvements, macro clarifications), build system fixes, and the major ocamllex exhaustiveness checking feature.
activity: **24 PRs merged** including major features, documentation improvements, and bug fixes
**18 new PRs opened** covering runtime optimizations, stdlib additions, and architecture improvements

**5 new issues reported** including a regression in 5.4.1 on aarch64 [#14574](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14574), GC pacing discussion [#14533](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14533), and class system exception [#14557](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14557)
**4 issues closed** including fixes for Pexp_struct_item regression [#14554](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14554) and GADT match warning [#14562](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14562)
repositories.discussions_summary: Active technical discussions around GC pacing and default parameters in 5.5, a regression in 5.4.1 causing assembler failures on aarch64, and questions about C API function declaration guarantees.
notable_discussions: **GC pacing and default parameters in 5.5** - Discussion about performance characteristics and tuning parameters for the garbage collector in upcoming releases. [#14533](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14533)

**Regression in 5.4.1 on aarch64** - Assembler failure with "Error: immediate out of range" when rebuilding libguestfs, traced to SELinux permissions. [#14574](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14574)
**C API function declarations guarantees** - Developer discussion about guarantees and contracts for C API function declarations. [#14516](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14516)
**Uncaught exception with parameterised classes** - Fatal error regression since 4.14.0 when using certain class patterns, being investigated. [#14557](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14557)
repositories.trends_summary: Strong focus on documentation quality and stdlib improvements with multiple PRs fixing typos and errors. Runtime improvements continue with GC enhancements and memory management optimizations. Growing attention to multi-architecture support (ppc64, s390x) and platform-specific optimizations.
emerging_trends: **Documentation quality initiative** - Multiple PRs (#14555, #14560, #14561, #14563, #14567) improving stdlib documentation accuracy, fixing typos, and adding missing @since annotations
**Runtime performance and clarity** - Ongoing work on GC improvements (#14365 Idle phase for major GC, #14571 Faster minor GC), memory management optimizations, and code clarity improvements
**Multi-architecture support** - Continued work on ppc64 (#14573), s390x (#14524, #14547), and aarch64 platforms with native implementations and fixes
**Build system modernization** - Improvements to configure scripts, dependency generation, and CI infrastructure (#14565 timeout increases)
**Type system refinements** - Work on modular explicits in let rec (#14537), type approximation refactoring (#14538), and fixing class-related edge cases
repositories.issues_summary: No good-first-issues were identified this week. Current open issues include architectural regressions, GC parameter discussions, and class system edge cases.
repositories.contributors_summary: Top contributors this week: Bozhidar Batsov (4 PRs on documentation), Damien Doligez (4 PRs on build/GC), Tim McGilchrist (3 PRs on ppc64 support), Gabriel Scherer (2 PRs on bug fixes), Nick Barnes (2 PRs on runtime), and Antonin Décimo (2 PRs on C2y/cleanup). repositories.brief_summary: Depexts performance optimization merged, moving availability checks to update time for faster package operations
repositories.new_features_summary: Depexts availability now computed during opam update, improving performance of install and show commands
repositories.new_features:
- **Depexts availability caching** implemented by [@arozovyk](https://github.com/arozovyk) moves system package availability checks to `opam update` time, eliminating redundant polling during `opam install` and `opam show` operations ([#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489))
- **Performance improvement** for `opam show` command, reducing execution time from ~2 seconds to <0.3 seconds by avoiding repeated depexts loading ([#4172](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4172))
- **Repository state caching** for system package availability status with automatic invalidation when OS family changes ([#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489))
- **Read-only detection** for opam root directory to detect sandbox environments, preventing cache updates during package build/install commands ([#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489))
- **Status messages** during `opam update` to inform users when system package availability is being computed ([#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489))
- **API enhancements** including new `OpamSysInteract` functions `available_packages` and `installed_packages` for separate polling, and `OpamSwitchState.update_sys_packages` for updating depexts status of package sets ([#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489))
- Updated **master_changes.md** documentation with detailed API changes and behavioral improvements ([edfc4c1b](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/commit/edfc4c1b7279be06b41e9bef25a6b8a32380b157))
repositories.activity_summary: PR opened for PATH hook precedence fix; depexts performance work merged; switch link test added
repositories.activity:
- **PATH precedence preservation** PR opened by [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) to update shell hook behavior, maintaining user-defined PATH ordering rather than always prepending opam switch bins ([#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859))
- **Depexts performance optimization** merged by [@arozovyk](https://github.com/arozovyk) with extensive changes across 39 files (1712 additions, 275 deletions), refactoring system package status polling to occur at update time rather than on-demand ([#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489))
- **State management improvements** including new `os_family` type moved to `OpamStateTypes`, `depexts` field replacing `no_depexts` in config, and `repo_syspkgs_available` field added to repository state cache ([#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489))
- **Test coverage expansion** with [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) adding switch link behavior test ([#6860](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6860)), plus multiple depexts tests for unknown OS families, disabled depexts scenarios, and system availability polling demonstrations ([#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489))
- **Issue resolution** closing long-standing performance issue [#4172](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4172) from 2020 about `opam show` slowness and [#6461](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6461) proposing depexts calculation at update time
- **Documentation typo fix** correcting depexts bypass note by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) ([4822b3e6](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/commit/4822b3e69d748eb3835e3f037b9d58b2c1897ecb))
repositories.discussions_summary: PATH hook behavior discussion and switch link behavior confirmation
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) proposed **PATH update behavior change** in shell hooks to preserve user-defined directory precedence rather than always prepending opam switch bins, addressing issue [#6815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6815); [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) confirmed the PR will be discussed in the next opam dev meeting ([#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859))

Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) demonstrated that the latest opam version has correct behavior via test in [#6860](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6860)
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) reviewed and merged [#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489), noting unrelated `depexts-alpine` CI failure to be fixed later, and provided code review feedback on test documentation ([#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489))
repositories.trends_summary: Performance optimization focus with caching strategies and extensive API refactoring for depexts handling
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Performance through caching** strategy demonstrated with depexts availability moved to update time, showing commitment to improving user experience by reducing repetitive system calls during common operations like `opam show` and `opam install`
- **Extensive API evolution** across opam-state library with `OpamSysInteract`, `OpamStateTypes`, `OpamRepositoryState`, and `OpamSwitchState` modules receiving substantial additions for depexts handling separation and caching infrastructure
- **Test-driven validation** approach with comprehensive test coverage for edge cases including unknown OS families, disabled depexts scenarios, and system availability polling behavior before merging functionality changes
- **User experience refinement** addressing long-standing issues from 2020 and improving shell integration behavior to respect user preferences while maintaining opam functionality

repositories.contributors:

- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) reviewed and merged both PRs ([#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489), [#6860](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6860)), provided code review feedback, and participated in issue closure decisions for [#4172](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4172) and [#6461](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6461)

Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) added test coverage for switch link behavior ([#6860](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6860)), fixed documentation typos ([4822b3e6](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/commit/4822b3e69d748eb3835e3f037b9d58b2c1897ecb)), contributed to depexts refactoring work, and responded to user reports ([#6857](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6857), [#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859))
- [Stephen Sherratt](https://github.com/gridbugs) opened PR to improve PATH hook behavior for preserving user directory precedence ([#6859](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6859))
- [Matthieu Sozeau](https://github.com/mattam82) reported switch link behavior issue requesting prevention of errors when linking over existing switches ([#6857](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6857))

repositories.brief_summary: Two issues opened addressing code action compatibility and hover functionality gaps
repositories.activity_summary: Discussion of code action editor compatibility and ongoing work on custom LSP requests
repositories.activity:
- [Clément Delafargue](https://github.com/divarvel) reported that the **construct an expression code action** fails in Helix editor with an `editor.action.triggerSuggest` command error ([#1588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1588)), prompting [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) to explain that this code action is currently **VSCode-specific** and suggest improving the implementation to generate the first construct result by default rather than relying on client commands
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) opened an issue to address **hover functionality gaps** after [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) identified missing cases where hover does not trigger on class inheritance syntax like `inherit [some_ty] some_class as super` ([#1587](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1587))
- **Custom LSP request for merlin-locate-types** remains under review with [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) addressing documentation feedback from [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1584))
- Automated **flake.lock dependency updates** continue to accumulate, with the latest updates including merlin and nixpkgs packages ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1437))
repositories.discussions_summary: Debate over client-specific code action implementation and suggestions for editor-agnostic fallbacks
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) proposed rethinking the **construct code action architecture** to avoid relying on client-specific commands like `editor.action.triggerSuggest`, suggesting that the action should generate the first construct result by default with optional custom requests for more precise behavior, similar to the approach used in ocaml-eglot ([#1588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1588))
- [Clément Delafargue](https://github.com/divarvel) suggested that code actions should only be advertised to clients that declare support for them in their LSP capabilities, though [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) clarified that **client commands are meant for UX enhancements** rather than core functionality, using the destruct command's "jump to next hole" behavior as an example where the command succeeds even if cursor movement is unavailable ([#1588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1588))
repositories.trends_summary: Focus on improving editor compatibility and filling gaps in LSP feature coverage
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Cross-editor compatibility** is becoming a priority, with issues highlighting that certain code actions and features work only in VSCode while other editors like Helix lack equivalent functionality ([#1588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1588))
- **Hover functionality refinement** continues as developers identify and document edge cases where type information tooltips fail to appear, particularly around object-oriented features like class inheritance ([#1587](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1587))
- **Custom LSP requests** are being developed to expose Merlin functionality more directly, allowing editors to build features beyond standard LSP capabilities ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1584))
repositories.contributors_summary: Xavier Van de Woestyne, Clément Delafargue, and Thomas Refis participated in issue discussions and PR reviews
repositories.contributors:
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) opened the hover reinforcement issue ([#1587](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1587)), participated in the construct expression code action discussion ([#1588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1588)), and updated documentation on the custom locate types request ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1584))
- [Clément Delafargue](https://github.com/divarvel) reported the construct expression code action failure in Helix and suggested capability-based feature advertisement ([#1588](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1588))
- [Thomas Refis](https://github.com/trefis) identified missing hover cases in class inheritance syntax that led to the hover reinforcement issue ([#1587](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1587))
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) reviewed the custom locate types PR and provided feedback on documentation and specification ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1584))

repositories.trends_summary: Focus on package management UX, OxCaml ecosystem support, and Rocq tooling enhancements
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Package management UX improvements** continue with portable lock directory refinements, better error messages, and relocatable compiler support reducing rebuild overhead
- **OxCaml ecosystem maturation** with parameterised library support for js_of_ocaml enabling more flexible compilation strategies
- **Rocq tooling expansion** with multiple extraction stanza support and expected output testing bringing testing capabilities closer to OCaml ecosystem standards

- **Internal code quality** focus on refactoring with Result type cleanup, annotation removal, and API simplification

repositories.brief_summary: Wasm performance optimizations merged, dynlink/toplevel support and native effects PRs opened, CI updated to OCaml 5.4.0
repositories.new_features_summary: Faster Wasm string conversions and array buffer copies, standard Wasm Text format, subtyping fixes, packed array access fix
repositories.new_features:
- Faster **copy operations** between Wasm strings and JavaScript array buffers improve runtime performance ([#2124](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2124), [a63b641](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/a63b6419aeff133d7c87b3ac92f2aa9f2075443f))

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with type system evolution, backend optimizations, and major Chamelon dune integration work
repositories.activity_summary: Type system enhancements including kind `any` support, morphisms work, backend optimizations, and 5.4 merge activities
repositories.activity:
- **New type system capabilities** with [Luke Maurer](https://github.com/lukemaurer) opening PR to allow fields and constructor args of kind `any` ([#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461)), changing `option` parameter kind to `any` ([#5462](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5462)), and [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) opening PRs for normal form morphisms ([#5453](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5453)) and explicit comparison functions for morphisms ([#5452](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5452))
- **Backend testing infrastructure** expanded by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) with 3,000+ lines of `[%%expect_asm]` tests for bad codegen cases ([#5447](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5447)), following merged support for expect_asm in tests ([#5350](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5350))
- **Static computations framework** added by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) with 1,200+ line implementation affecting 51 files ([#5430](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5430))
- **Upstream 5.4 merge work** progressing through [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) with conflicts resolved in utils ([#5442](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5442)), separation of middle/backend PR diffs ([#5460](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5460)), and resolution of middle/backend conflicts ([#5463](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5463))
- **Runtime improvements** with [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opening PRs for ephemeron marking fix ([#5450](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5450)) and sizeclasses.h dependency ([#5455](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5455)), plus [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) fixing ocamldebug's debug_printers and adding CI test-runs ([#5465](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5465))
- **Chamelon dune integration** merged by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) with nearly 2,000 line addition across 19 files ([#5423](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5423))
- **Backend spill optimization** completed by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) fixing useless spills across 37 files ([#5429](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5429)) with reproduction case added ([#5432](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5432))
- **Type system refinements** merged including redundant modality warning by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) affecting 22 files ([#5373](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5373)), pattern variable refactoring to inline records by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([#5437](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5437)), meet/join terminology regularization by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) ([#5451](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5451)), and variable pair renaming from 0-indexed to 1-indexed ([#5445](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5445))
- **Unboxed types bug fixes** with [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) fixing bytecode deep copying of unboxed product fields ([#5386](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5386)) and [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) preventing effects duplication in `%makearray_dynamic` ([#5459](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5459))
- **Compiler infrastructure** improvements including removal of thread-local dynamic bindings support by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) ([#5171](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5171)), Data Flow lifted constants internalization by [Guillaume Bury](https://github.com/Gbury) ([#5368](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5368)), and multiple documentation/tooling fixes by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#5468](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5468), [#5467](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5467), [#5448](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5448), [#5435](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5435), [#5434](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5434), [#5433](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5433))
- **Bug fixes** delivered by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) for coerce typing error printing ([#5457](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5457)), [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) for verbose jkind printing ([#5449](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5449)), [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) for comonadic_to_monadic_X ([#5446](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5446)), [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) for degraded value slots types ([#5427](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5427)), and [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) removing unnecessary runtime call ([#5454](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5454))
- **Performance optimizations** including [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) optimizing Sort.set to avoid closure allocation ([#5443](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5443)), [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) short-circuiting common case in instance_poly_for_jkind ([#5406](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5406)), and [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) ensuring proper interrupt initialization ([#5424](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5424))
- **Fexpr parser work** by [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) removing indexed menhir/ocamllex files ([#5440](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5440)) and adding support for small int and naked floats blocks ([#5441](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5441))

- **Test infrastructure** enhanced by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) with directive for marking test environment as top-level for staging checks ([#5464](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5464))

repositories.brief_summary: Compiler upgrade to 5.2.0minus-31 merged alongside punned record pattern locate fix
repositories.new_features_summary: Fixed locate behavior for punned record patterns and upgraded compiler to 5.2.0minus-31
repositories.new_features:
- **Locate fix for punned record patterns** by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) enables proper navigation in code using record field punning syntax like `let f { a; b } = ...` where clicking on `a` in the pattern now correctly jumps to the label definition rather than incorrectly reporting "already at definition" ([#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147))
- **Compiler upgrade to 5.2.0minus-31** brings upstream OCaml changes including Format_doc module integration, abstract jkind support, and enhanced type system features with corresponding updates to type printing and error reporting ([#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
- **Parser attribute mechanism** for punned record patterns added to distinguish between pattern and label nodes in the typed tree, enabling accurate code navigation tools ([0d9b939](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/commit/0d9b939d8f9e321c7c2b1d798cd1d2542faf9588))
- **Type recovery improvements** for constructor representation handling with new test coverage ensuring Merlin correctly handles abstract types in module construction scenarios ([3f78528](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/commit/3f78528986454c4cb91c3d1a2f6c591f28982a75))
repositories.activity_summary: One open PR for type recovery improvement and one awaiting CI fixes for signature help tests
repositories.activity:
- **Type recovery enhancement** opened by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) to fix error handling in function applications with mismatched types, preventing Merlin from discarding typed function identifiers when argument types don't match ([#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/222))
- **Signature help test suite** added by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) to ensure proper functioning with only labelled arguments, currently awaiting CI whitespace fixes ([#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205))
- **Compiler merge completed** with [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) successfully integrating 5.2.0minus-31 after resolving Format_doc and abstract jkind conflicts, with review from [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) requesting additional test coverage for new features ([#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
- **Punned record pattern fix merged** after rebasing from June 2025, implementing parser-level attributes to improve locate behavior following the same approach used earlier for punned let expressions ([#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147), [#96](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/96))
- **CI debugging efforts** concluded with [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) closing the experimental PR used to investigate CI issues affecting the main branch ([#220](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/220))
- **Draft merge PR closed** for 5.2.0minus-29 as superseded by the completed 5.2.0minus-31 merge ([#211](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/211))
repositories.discussions_summary: Code review discussions on test coverage and upstream synchronization plans
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) requested **test coverage expansion** for new compiler features introduced in the 5.2.0minus-31 merge, particularly around abstract jkinds and Format_doc integration, with [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) adding tests for constructor representation handling ([#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
- Discussion of **upstream contribution plans** for both the punned record pattern fix and the earlier punned let expression feature, with acknowledgment from [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) that these improvements could benefit the upstream Merlin project ([#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147), [#96](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/96))
- **Type variable naming behavior change** analyzed in detail for the compiler merge, where switching from lazy printer to eagerly-evaluated Format_doc.t changed the timing of type variable name assignment, affecting test output for class declarations ([#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) created **internal tickets** (6599, 6600, 6601) to track follow-up work for Jkind.is_value_for_printing workarounds and other temporary solutions introduced during the compiler merge ([#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on compiler synchronization and navigation feature improvements
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Regular compiler synchronization** continues with the oxcaml/merlin fork tracking upstream compiler changes through systematic import and merge processes, with this week completing the 5.2.0minus-31 integration after handling multiple retags ([#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221), [#211](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/211))
- **Editor integration refinements** for code navigation features, building on the punned let expression work from fall 2025 with the new punned record pattern support showing a pattern of systematic improvements to Merlin's AST navigation accuracy ([#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147), [#96](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/96))
- **Type recovery robustness** improvements emerging as a focus area, with the new PR addressing error handling in application expressions to preserve more type information for editor queries even when type errors occur ([#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/222))
- **Test-driven development** approach evident in both the punned record pattern fix and compiler merge, with reviewers consistently requesting test coverage for new functionality and changes ([#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147), [#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
repositories.contributors_summary: Liam Stevenson drove compiler upgrade and navigation fixes; Richard Eisenberg provided code review
repositories.contributors:

- [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) provided comprehensive code review on the compiler merge PR with detailed questions about test coverage, Compat shim implementation, and type recovery edge cases, requesting additional tests for new features ([#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))

repositories.brief_summary: Two issues opened discussing error handling improvements and temporary directory utilities
repositories.activity_summary: Two proposals opened for improving server error handling and adding temporary directory support
repositories.activity:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) opened issue proposing improved **error handling in `Eio.Net.run_server`** when `accept` fails, including discussion of semaphore management, default `max_connections` values, and potential `on_accept_error` callback ([#830](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/830))
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) proposed adding **temporary directory utilities** including `Stdenv.tmp` capability and `Path.open_temp_dir`/`Path.with_temp_dir` functions for managing temporary directories ([#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831))
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on run_server error handling addressing DoS concerns and best practices
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) responded to the `run_server` error handling proposal, noting that **connection limits are inherently vulnerable** to DoS attacks and suggesting that crashing and restarting may be preferable to waiting indefinitely when FD leaks occur, while supporting an optional `on_accept_error` callback and recommending documentation on best practices for public-facing servers ([#830](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/830))
repositories.contributors_summary: Volker Diels-Grabsch opened two proposals and Thomas Leonard provided design feedback
repositories.contributors:

- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) provided detailed feedback on the `run_server` error handling proposal, addressing security considerations and implementation approaches ([#830](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/830))

group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune OxCaml jsoo and Rocq improvements, opam depexts caching, js_of_ocaml Wasm optimizations, Merlin signature help fixes
group_summaries.new_features:
- **OxCaml js_of_ocaml support** in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) for parameterised libraries enabling separate compilation by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) ([ocaml/dune#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414))
- **C stubs dependency tracking** in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) ensures proper rebuilds when switching OCaml compilers through header file dependencies ([ocaml/dune#13652](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13652))
- **Package management depext hints** in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) now display system dependencies in space-delimited format for easy copy-paste by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) ([ocaml/dune#13636](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13636))
- **Multiple Rocq extraction stanzas** in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) now supported in a single directory by [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai), fixing [ocaml/dune#8042](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/8042) ([ocaml/dune#13531](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13531))
- **Depexts availability caching** in [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) implemented by [@arozovyk](https://github.com/arozovyk) moves system package availability checks to `opam update` time, eliminating redundant polling during install and show operations ([ocaml/opam#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489))
- **Performance improvement** in [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) for `opam show` command, reducing execution time from ~2 seconds to <0.3 seconds ([ocaml/opam#4172](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4172))
- **Faster Wasm operations** in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) including copy operations between Wasm strings and JavaScript array buffers, and faster small string conversions by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2124](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2124))
- **Wasm runtime modernization** in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) including standard Wasm Text format, subtyping fixes, packed array access fixes, and multiple start functions support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2154](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2154))
- **Signature help improvements** in [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) for labelled and optional parameters by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo), ensuring correct parameter matching when arguments are already provided ([ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) merged OxCaml js_of_ocaml support, C stub dependency fixes, and Rocq extraction improvements while advancing portable lock directory work ([ocaml/dune#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414), [ocaml/dune#13652](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13652), [ocaml/dune#13531](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13531))

- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) merged Wasm runtime optimizations and opened dynlink/toplevel support and native effects PRs ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2124](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2124), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189))

- **Wasm native effects** in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) PR opened by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) adding 369 lines across 31 files ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2189](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2189))
- **Variable coalescing optimization** in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) under review showing 74-91% register reduction and 10-16% compressed size reduction ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2166](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2166))
- **Let..in binding fixes** in [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) PR opened by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) for signature help, under review with feedback from [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) and [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) ([ocaml/merlin#2036](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2036))
- **Enclosing command -stop flag** in [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) enabling region-based queries for better type annotation and locate-type functionality ([ocaml/merlin#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029))
- **Custom LSP request for merlin-locate-types** in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) under review with [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) addressing documentation feedback ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1584))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:

- **Shared editor integration work** by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) across [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) region-based queries ([ocaml/merlin#2029](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2029)) and [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) custom requests ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/pull/1584))

group_summaries.notable_discussions:

- **Wasm backend advancement** in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) with runtime modernization, toplevel support, native effects, and code size optimizations spanning multiple PRs
- **Package management UX refinement** in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) and [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) with portable lock directories, depext improvements, and shell integration enhancements
- **Rocq tooling expansion** in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) with multiple extraction stanza support and expected output testing bringing capabilities closer to OCaml ecosystem standards ([ocaml/dune#13531](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13531), [ocaml/dune#13632](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13632))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Type system evolution and compiler synchronization dominated with layout polymorphism advances and backend testing expansion
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Merlin gained punned record pattern navigation and compiler 5.2.0minus-31 integration with enhanced type system support
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Punned record pattern navigation in Merlin** by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) enables accurate locate behavior for code like `let f { a; b } = ...` where clicking on `a` now correctly navigates to the label definition rather than incorrectly reporting "already at definition" ([oxcaml/merlin#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147))
- **Merlin compiler upgrade to 5.2.0minus-31** brings upstream changes including Format_doc module integration, abstract jkind support, and enhanced type system features with corresponding updates to type printing and error reporting ([oxcaml/merlin#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
- **Parser attribute mechanism for punned patterns** added to distinguish between pattern and label nodes in the typed tree, enabling accurate code navigation tools ([oxcaml/merlin commit 0d9b939](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/commit/0d9b939d8f9e321c7c2b1d798cd1d2542faf9588))
- **Type recovery improvements in Merlin** for constructor representation handling with new test coverage ensuring correct handling of abstract types in module construction scenarios ([oxcaml/merlin commit 3f78528](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/commit/3f78528986454c4cb91c3d1a2f6c591f28982a75))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **Primary activity concentrated in oxcaml/oxcaml** with 16 contributors merging code focused on type system evolution, backend optimizations, and upstream OCaml 5.4 merge preparation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5442](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5442), [#5460](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5460), [#5463](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5463))
- **Merlin development active** with compiler synchronization to 5.2.0minus-31 and editor navigation improvements for punned patterns ([oxcaml/merlin#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221), [#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147))
- **Three repositories inactive** with no recorded activity in oxcaml/opam-repository, oxcaml/js_of_ocaml, or janestreet/opam-repository during this period
- **Type system work dominates** with layout polymorphism enhancements including kind `any` support for fields and constructor arguments ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461), [#5462](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5462))
- **Backend testing infrastructure expansion** with 3,000+ lines of expect_asm tests for codegen verification ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5447](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5447))
- **Runtime improvements** including ephemeron marking fixes and ocamldebug enhancements with CI testing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5450](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5450), [#5465](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5465))
group_summaries.activity_summary: Compiler infrastructure work including Chamelon dune integration, static computations framework, and extensive bug fixes across type system and backend
group_summaries.activity:
- **Chamelon dune integration merged** by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) with nearly 2,000 line addition across 19 files enabling dune build system support for the Chamelon tool ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5423](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5423))
- **Static computations framework** added by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) with 1,200+ line implementation affecting 51 files to enable compile-time optimizations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5430](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5430))
- **Backend spill optimization** completed by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) fixing useless spills across 37 files with reproduction case ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5429](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5429), [#5432](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5432))
- **Morphisms work progressing** with [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) opening PRs for normal form morphisms and explicit comparison functions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5453](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5453), [#5452](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5452))
- **Backend testing expansion** by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) with 3,000+ lines of expect_asm tests for bad codegen cases following merged support for expect_asm infrastructure ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5447](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5447), [#5350](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5350))
- **Type system refinements** including redundant modality warning by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) affecting 22 files, pattern variable refactoring to inline records by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn), and meet/join terminology regularization by [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5373](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5373), [#5437](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5437), [#5451](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5451))
- **Unboxed types bug fixes** with [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) fixing bytecode deep copying of unboxed product fields and [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat) preventing effects duplication in `%makearray_dynamic` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5386](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5386), [#5459](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5459))
- **Runtime improvements** with [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) opening PRs for ephemeron marking fix and sizeclasses.h dependency, plus [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) fixing ocamldebug's debug_printers with CI test-runs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5450](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5450), [#5455](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5455), [#5465](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5465))
- **Compiler infrastructure cleanup** including removal of thread-local dynamic bindings support by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark), Data Flow lifted constants internalization by [Guillaume Bury](https://github.com/Gbury), and multiple documentation/tooling fixes by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5171](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5171), [#5368](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5368), [#5468](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5468))
- **Performance optimizations** including [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) optimizing Sort.set to avoid closure allocation and [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) short-circuiting common case in instance_poly_for_jkind ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5443](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5443), [#5406](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5406))
- **Merlin type recovery enhancement** opened by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) to fix error handling in function applications with mismatched types, preventing discarding of typed function identifiers ([oxcaml/merlin#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/222))
- **Ongoing draft work** includes [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) making Stdlib mostly stateless, [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) lowering inlined always attribute priority, and [Guillaume Bury](https://github.com/Gbury) fixing Debuginfo.to_location ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5458](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5458), [#5439](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5439), [#5444](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5444))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Compiler version synchronization** between oxcaml/oxcaml and oxcaml/merlin with Merlin tracking compiler changes through 5.2.0minus-31 merge that includes Format_doc and abstract jkind features being actively developed in the main compiler ([oxcaml/merlin#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461))
- **Type system features flowing from compiler to Merlin** with abstract jkind support and enhanced type printing requiring corresponding updates in Merlin's type recovery and display logic ([oxcaml/merlin#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
- **Upstream OCaml 5.4 merge preparation** in oxcaml/oxcaml by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) resolving conflicts across compiler subsystems will eventually require corresponding Merlin updates ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5442](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5442), [#5460](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5460), [#5463](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5463))
- **Editor integration improvements in Merlin** depend on compiler AST structure with punned pattern support following earlier punned let expression work, both requiring compiler-level parser changes ([oxcaml/merlin#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147), [#96](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/96))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Test coverage expansion requested** by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) on Merlin compiler merge for abstract jkinds and Format_doc integration, with [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) adding tests for constructor representation handling ([oxcaml/merlin#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
- **Upstream contribution plans** discussed for Merlin's punned pattern navigation fixes, with acknowledgment that both punned record patterns and earlier punned let expression features could benefit the upstream Merlin project ([oxcaml/merlin#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147), [#96](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/96))
- **Type variable naming behavior change** analyzed in detail for Merlin compiler merge, where switching from lazy printer to eagerly-evaluated Format_doc.t changed timing of type variable name assignment, affecting test output for class declarations ([oxcaml/merlin#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
- **Internal tracking tickets created** by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) (tickets 6599, 6600, 6601) for follow-up work on Jkind.is_value_for_printing workarounds and other temporary solutions introduced during the compiler merge ([oxcaml/merlin#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
- **Inlining bugs identified** by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls) requiring investigation of heuristic behavior and attribute priority handling ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5438](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5438), [#5439](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5439))
- **Numeric conversion regression reported** by [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) in fexpr parser requiring investigation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5431](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5431))
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Layout polymorphism advancement** with concentrated work on kind `any` support for fields and constructor arguments, enabling more flexible type system capabilities for unboxed and custom representations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461), [#5462](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5462))
- **Backend testing maturity** reflected in comprehensive expect_asm test suite development for assembly-level verification, enabling systematic regression testing of code generation quality ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5447](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5447), [#5350](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5350))
- **Upstream synchronization ramping up** with multiple PRs resolving OCaml 5.4 merge conflicts across compiler subsystems (utils, middle-end, backend), indicating preparation for major version integration ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5442](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5442), [#5460](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5460), [#5463](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5463))
- **Editor integration refinements** continuing in Merlin with systematic improvements to AST navigation accuracy, building on earlier punned let expression work with new punned record pattern support ([oxcaml/merlin#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147), [#96](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/96))
- **Type system modernization** through morphisms normalization, modality system refinements, and terminology regularization reflecting ongoing type theory evolution ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5453](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5453), [#5452](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5452), [#5451](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5451))
- **Unboxed types stabilization** continuing with bytecode handling fixes and effects system integration corrections as the feature matures ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5386](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5386), [#5459](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5459))
- **Static analysis capabilities expanding** with new framework for static computations enabling compile-time optimizations and constant propagation improvements ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5430](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5430))
- **Test-driven development approach** evident in both Merlin and oxcaml/oxcaml with reviewers consistently requesting test coverage for new functionality and changes ([oxcaml/merlin#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221), [oxcaml/merlin#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147))

group_summaries.brief_summary: 24 PRs merged with exhaustiveness checking for ocamllex, new Array fold functions, and GC improvements.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Exhaustiveness checking for ocamllex, Array.fold_left2/fold_right2, runtime macros, and Iarray API updates.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Exhaustiveness checking for ocamllex regular expressions** - If a rule does not match some input, an example of nonmatching input is shown in a warning message. The new warning can be disabled using `-w -missing-case` ([ocaml/ocaml#14448](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14448))
- **Array.fold_left2 and Array.fold_right2 functions** - New standard library functions that fold over two arrays simultaneously, raising Invalid_argument if arrays have different lengths ([ocaml/ocaml#14553](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14553))
- **Improved runtime clarity with Scannable_* macros** - Replaced all comparisons against No_scan_tag with macros for better legibility and correctness, fixing one bug in weak.c ([ocaml/ocaml#14493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14493))
- **Iarray API consistency** - Updated Iarray module to expose 'a Iarray.t in function types rather than 'a iarray, making the built-in iarray type an implementation detail ([ocaml/ocaml#14576](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14576))
- **Improved installation time in opam** - Shrinking Git-generated source archives to reduce installation time ([ocaml/ocaml#14152](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14152))
- **Fix for --disable-unix-lib build** - ocamltest now builds correctly when configured with --disable-unix-lib ([ocaml/ocaml#14558](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14558))
- **Linux ppc64 architecture detection** - Proper detection for Linux distros targeting ELFv1 (like Debian), correctly setting the arch configure variable ([ocaml/ocaml#14573](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14573))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **24 PRs merged** in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) with major features including exhaustiveness checking for ocamllex ([ocaml/ocaml#14448](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14448)), new Array functions ([ocaml/ocaml#14553](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14553)), and GC improvements ([ocaml/ocaml#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365))
- **18 new PRs opened** covering runtime optimizations, stdlib additions, and architecture improvements in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml)
- **5 new issues reported** including a regression in 5.4.1 on aarch64 ([ocaml/ocaml#14574](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14574)) and GC pacing discussion ([ocaml/ocaml#14533](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14533))
- **Documentation maintenance** in [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) with improved contributor image rendering in Committee.md ([ocaml/RFCs#61](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/61))
- **Strong community participation** with contributions from [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) (4 PRs), [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) (4 PRs), [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) (3 PRs), and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) (2 PRs)
group_summaries.activity_summary: Documentation improvements, runtime enhancements, GC optimizations, and multi-architecture support work.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Documentation quality initiative** - Multiple PRs fixing stdlib documentation with [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) improving accuracy, fixing typos, and adding missing @since annotations ([ocaml/ocaml#14555](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14555), [ocaml/ocaml#14560](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14560), [ocaml/ocaml#14561](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14561), [ocaml/ocaml#14563](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14563))
- **Runtime performance work** - [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) merged major GC Idle phase improvements ([ocaml/ocaml#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365)) and ongoing faster minor GC work ([ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571))
- **Multi-architecture support** - [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) advanced ppc64 architecture detection ([ocaml/ocaml#14573](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14573)) and FreeBSD frame pointer support ([ocaml/ocaml#14486](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14486), [ocaml/ocaml#14530](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14530))
- **Bug fixes** - [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) fixed Pexp_struct_item regression ([ocaml/ocaml#14556](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14556)) and GC ephemeron marking ([ocaml/ocaml#14338](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14338))
- **Build system improvements** - [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) fixed --disable-unix-lib build ([ocaml/ocaml#14558](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14558)), increased CI timeouts ([ocaml/ocaml#14565](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14565)), and cleaned up tests ([ocaml/ocaml#14549](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14549))
- **Type system refinements** - Work on modular explicits in let rec ([ocaml/ocaml#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537)) and type approximation refactoring ([ocaml/ocaml#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538))
- **Code modernization** - [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) adopted C2y countof usage ([ocaml/ocaml#14546](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14546)) and removed deprecated fatal error functions ([ocaml/ocaml#14559](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14559))
- **Parser improvements** - [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) reimplemented let open/module/exception ([ocaml/ocaml#13839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13839)) and [Samuel Vivien](https://github.com/samsa1) distinguished module patterns from type constraints ([ocaml/ocaml#14149](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14149))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Shared contributor activity** - [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) contributed both to [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) with dune improvements ([ocaml/ocaml#14408](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14408)) and to [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) with committee documentation fixes ([ocaml/RFCs#61](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/61))
- **Review coordination** - [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) actively reviewed and merged work across both repositories, contributing to [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) parser improvements ([ocaml/ocaml#13839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13839)) and maintaining [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) documentation ([ocaml/RFCs#61](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/61))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **GC pacing and default parameters in 5.5** - Active discussion about performance characteristics and tuning parameters for the garbage collector in upcoming releases ([ocaml/ocaml#14533](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14533))
- **Regression in 5.4.1 on aarch64** - Investigation of assembler failure with "Error: immediate out of range" when rebuilding libguestfs, traced to SELinux permissions ([ocaml/ocaml#14574](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14574))
- **C API function declarations guarantees** - Developer discussion about guarantees and contracts for C API function declarations ([ocaml/ocaml#14516](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14516))
- **Uncaught exception with parameterised classes** - Fatal error regression since 4.14.0 when using certain class patterns, under investigation ([ocaml/ocaml#14557](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14557))
- **GitHub avatar image rendering** - Technical discussion between [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) and [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) about GitHub's inconsistent handling of size parameters for avatar images ([ocaml/RFCs#61](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/61))
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Documentation quality focus** - Multiple PRs across the week improving stdlib documentation accuracy, with [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) leading efforts to fix typos and add missing @since annotations ([ocaml/ocaml#14555](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14555), [ocaml/ocaml#14560](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14560), [ocaml/ocaml#14561](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14561), [ocaml/ocaml#14563](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14563), [ocaml/ocaml#14567](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14567))
- **Runtime performance and clarity** - Continued work on GC improvements with Idle phase for major GC ([ocaml/ocaml#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365)), faster minor GC ([ocaml/ocaml#14571](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14571)), and code clarity improvements with Scannable_* macros ([ocaml/ocaml#14493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14493))
- **Multi-architecture expansion** - Growing support for ppc64 ([ocaml/ocaml#14573](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14573)), s390x ([ocaml/ocaml#14524](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14524), [ocaml/ocaml#14547](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14547)), and aarch64 platforms with native implementations and architecture-specific fixes
- **Build system modernization** - Ongoing improvements to configure scripts, dependency generation, and CI infrastructure with timeout increases ([ocaml/ocaml#14565](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14565))
- **Type system refinements** - Active work on modular explicits in let rec ([ocaml/ocaml#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14537)), type approximation refactoring ([ocaml/ocaml#14538](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14538)), and fixing class-related edge cases ([ocaml/ocaml#14557](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14557))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Focus on improving eio server robustness and file system utilities
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity concentrated in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) with two proposals addressing server error handling and temporary directory management
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) initiated both discussions, receiving detailed feedback from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) on the server error handling proposal ([ocaml-multicore/eio#830](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/830))
- No activity reported in [ocaml-multicore/domainslib](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/domainslib), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring), [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio), [ocaml-multicore/saturn](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/saturn), [ocaml-multicore/eio-trace](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio-trace), [ocaml-multicore/picos](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/picos), [ocaml-multicore/kcas](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/kcas), or [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-iomux](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-iomux)
group_summaries.activity_summary: Two proposals for eio covering server error handling and temporary directory utilities
group_summaries.activity:
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) proposed improving error handling in `Eio.Net.run_server` when `accept` fails, including discussion of semaphore management, default `max_connections` values, and potential `on_accept_error` callback ([ocaml-multicore/eio#830](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/830))
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) proposed adding temporary directory utilities including `Stdenv.tmp` capability and `Path.open_temp_dir`/`Path.with_temp_dir` functions for managing temporary directories ([ocaml-multicore/eio#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831))
group_summaries.notable_discussions: - [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) responded to the `run_server` error handling proposal in [ocaml-multicore/eio#830](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/830), noting that connection limits are inherently vulnerable to DoS attacks and suggesting that crashing and restarting may be preferable to waiting indefinitely when FD leaks occur, while supporting an optional `on_accept_error` callback and recommending documentation on best practices for public-facing servers
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- Discussion of security considerations in server error handling, particularly regarding DoS vulnerabilities and file descriptor leak management in [ocaml-multicore/eio#830](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/830)
- Interest in expanding eio's file system utilities with temporary directory support ([ocaml-multicore/eio#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831))

weekly_summary.brief_summary: OCaml 5.4.1 and 4.14.3 released with bug fixes, 67 opam packages added, OxCaml advances type system and Merlin integration.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: OCaml releases 5.4.1/4.14.3, exhaustiveness checking for ocamllex, Array.fold_left2/fold_right2, opam depexts caching, js_of_ocaml Wasm optimizations.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **OCaml 5.4.1 released** by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) providing bug fixes to the stable OCaml 5 series ([ocaml/ocaml releases](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/releases/tag/5.4.1))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **OCaml 4.14.3 released** by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) continuing support for the OCaml 4.14 maintenance branch with bug fixes ([ocaml/ocaml releases](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/releases/tag/4.14.3))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Exhaustiveness checking for ocamllex regular expressions** with new warning showing examples of nonmatching input when rules don't cover all inputs, controllable with `-w -missing-case` flag ([ocaml/ocaml#14448](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14448))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Array.fold_left2 and Array.fold_right2 functions** added to standard library for folding over two arrays simultaneously, raising Invalid_argument if arrays have different lengths ([ocaml/ocaml#14553](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14553))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Improved runtime clarity with Scannable_* macros** replacing all comparisons against No_scan_tag for better legibility and correctness, fixing one bug in weak.c ([ocaml/ocaml#14493](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14493))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Iarray API consistency** updating Iarray module to expose 'a Iarray.t in function types rather than 'a iarray, making the built-in iarray type an implementation detail ([ocaml/ocaml#14576](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14576))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Improved installation time in opam** by shrinking Git-generated source archives to reduce installation time ([ocaml/ocaml#14152](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14152))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Fix for --disable-unix-lib build** allowing ocamltest to build correctly when configured with --disable-unix-lib ([ocaml/ocaml#14558](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14558))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Linux ppc64 architecture detection** for Linux distros targeting ELFv1 (like Debian), correctly setting the arch configure variable ([ocaml/ocaml#14573](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14573))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Punned record pattern navigation in Merlin** by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) enabling accurate locate behavior for code like `let f { a; b } = ...` where clicking on `a` now correctly navigates to the label definition ([oxcaml/merlin#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Merlin compiler upgrade to 5.2.0minus-31** bringing upstream changes including Format_doc module integration, abstract jkind support, and enhanced type system features with corresponding updates to type printing and error reporting ([oxcaml/merlin#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **67 new packages added to opam-repository** including charrua, containers, flux, frama-clang, gen_js_api, grace, httpcats, hxd, ipaddr, and many others across 34 PRs ([ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Security documentation** on ocaml.org now includes direct link to SRT reports on GitHub ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3516](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3516))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Planet feed sources** on ocaml.org updated to disable defunct feeds (ujamjar, coq) and add Rocq Prover as new source ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3514](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3514))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml js_of_ocaml support** in Dune for parameterised libraries enabling separate compilation by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) ([ocaml/dune#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **C stubs dependency tracking** in Dune by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) ensuring proper rebuilds when switching OCaml compilers through header file dependencies ([ocaml/dune#13652](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13652))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Package management depext hints** in Dune now display system dependencies in space-delimited format for easy copy-paste by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) ([ocaml/dune#13636](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13636))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Multiple Rocq extraction stanzas** in Dune now supported in a single directory by [Rodolphe Lepigre](https://github.com/rlepigre-skylabs-ai), fixing [ocaml/dune#8042](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/8042) ([ocaml/dune#13531](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13531))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Depexts availability caching** in opam implemented by [@arozovyk](https://github.com/arozovyk) moving system package availability checks to `opam update` time, eliminating redundant polling during install and show operations ([ocaml/opam#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Performance improvement** in opam for `opam show` command, reducing execution time from ~2 seconds to <0.3 seconds ([ocaml/opam#4172](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/4172))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Faster Wasm operations** in js_of_ocaml including copy operations between Wasm strings and JavaScript array buffers, and faster small string conversions by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2124](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2124))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Wasm runtime modernization** in js_of_ocaml including standard Wasm Text format, subtyping fixes, packed array access fixes, and multiple start functions support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2154](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2154))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Signature help improvements** in Merlin for labelled and optional parameters by [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo), ensuring correct parameter matching when arguments are already provided ([ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032))

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:core__ The **OCaml compiler** released versions **5.4.1** and **4.14.3** with bug fixes, merged 24 PRs including major features like exhaustiveness checking for ocamllex ([ocaml/ocaml#14448](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14448)), new Array fold functions ([ocaml/ocaml#14553](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14553)), and GC improvements ([ocaml/ocaml#14365](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14365)); 18 new PRs were opened covering runtime optimizations, stdlib additions, and architecture improvements, with community participation from [Bozhidar Batsov](https://github.com/bbatsov) (4 PRs), [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez) (4 PRs), [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) (3 PRs), and [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) (2 PRs).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ The **OxCaml compiler** focused on **type system evolution** and **compiler synchronization** with 16 contributors merging work on layout polymorphism advances including kind `any` support for fields and constructor arguments ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5462](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5462)); **Merlin development** remained active with compiler synchronization to 5.2.0minus-31 and editor navigation improvements for punned patterns ([oxcaml/merlin#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221), [oxcaml/merlin#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147)); backend testing infrastructure expanded with 3,000+ lines of expect_asm tests for codegen verification ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5447](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5447)).
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ The **multicore group** had limited activity concentrated in eio with two proposals addressing server error handling and temporary directory management by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog), receiving detailed feedback from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) on the server error handling proposal ([ocaml-multicore/eio#830](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/830)).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ The **ecosystem repositories** had an active week with **67 new packages** added to opam-repository across 34 PRs with contributions from 34 community members, and focused on content infrastructure improvements including planet feed maintenance and security documentation updates; [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) contributed across both repositories with package updates in opam-repository and security documentation improvements in ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3516](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3516)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ The **development tools** led activity with [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) merging OxCaml js_of_ocaml support, C stub dependency fixes, and Rocq extraction improvements in Dune ([ocaml/dune#13414](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13414), [ocaml/dune#13652](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13652)); opam merged depexts performance optimization moving availability checks to update time, closing a 2020-era performance issue ([ocaml/opam#6489](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6489)); js_of_ocaml merged Wasm runtime optimizations and opened dynlink/toplevel support and native effects PRs ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2124](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2124), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2187](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2187)); Merlin merged signature help fixes and opened PRs for let..in binding improvements ([ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032), [ocaml/merlin#2036](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2036)).

oxcaml: -The **OxCaml compiler** focused on **type system evolution** and **compiler synchronization** with 16 contributors merging work on layout polymorphism advances including kind `any` support for fields and constructor arguments ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5461](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5461), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5462](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5462)); **Merlin development** remained active with compiler synchronization to 5.2.0minus-31 and editor navigation improvements for punned patterns ([oxcaml/merlin#221](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/221), [oxcaml/merlin#147](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/147)); backend testing infrastructure expanded with 3,000+ lines of expect_asm tests for codegen verification ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5447](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5447)).
multicore: -The **multicore group** had limited activity concentrated in eio with two proposals addressing server error handling and temporary directory management by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog), receiving detailed feedback from [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) on the server error handling proposal ([ocaml-multicore/eio#830](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/830)).

weekly_summary.activity_summary: Documentation improvements, runtime enhancements, GC optimizations, multi-architecture support, compiler infrastructure work, extensive package updates.
